critic_raw:
Verdict — ceramic defect classifier evaluation:

Overall accuracy at 1.4% prevalence cannot demonstrate defect-detection quality. The model may or may not be detecting defects — the metric doesn't tell us. Given the paper's stated asymmetric cost structure (escapes >> false rejections), the evaluation should have used recall or a cost-weighted metric. No defect-class performance is reported anywhere in the headline results or executive summary.

The deployment decision (replace visual rule before product launch) requires evidence that the model detects defects more reliably than the current rule. That evidence is not present.

Verdict: critique_wins
adjudication_raw: Baseline run 3. All three must-find issues identified. Verdict: critique_wins.
